task:
Harden the Oscar-Deb repair dialogue's DEB-TURN output parser against prose-wrapped JSON. This is the symmetric counterpart of the exact defect class fixed for ticket 0084's Oscar-evaluation turn.

BACKGROUND (read first, do not re-fix the Oscar turn):
- Ticket 0084's Oscar-side fix is ALREADY shipped and green: packages/daemon/src/oscar-deb-repair.ts has `parseOscarEvaluationArtifact(output, dialogueId)` (~line 212) which tolerates prose-wrapped JSON by scanning JSON object candidates via the private `jsonObjectCandidates` helper (~line 271); launcher.ts (~line 1502) already calls it. Tests pass. DO NOT change that behavior.
- The symmetric counterpart is NOT fixed: `parseDebTurnOutput` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~line 1803) still calls `JSON.parse(output)` directly on the Deb turn's output. So when the Deb turn emits a correct `applied`/`proposal` JSON object wrapped in prose (the same common LLM shape that broke the Oscar turn), the dialogue 500s identically with 'malformed artifact'.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. ONE OWNER ONLY for the prose-tolerant extraction. Do NOT write a second JSON-extraction routine anywhere. Factor the candidate-scan logic that `parseOscarEvaluationArtifact` already uses into a small shared, exported helper in oscar-deb-repair.ts (e.g. `firstJsonObjectArtifact(output): Record<string, unknown>` that reuses the existing private `jsonObjectCandidates` and tries candidates last-to-first, throwing a clear 'no JSON object artifact found' when none parse). Refactor `parseOscarEvaluationArtifact` to use this shared helper so there is exactly one extraction implementation.
2. Update `parseDebTurnOutput` (launcher.ts) to obtain its parsed object via that shared helper instead of raw `JSON.parse(output)`. Keep its existing normalization unchanged (the dialogueId injection and the kind==='applied' commit placeholder of {sha:'pending',committedPaths:[],outOfLanePaths:[]}); only the raw parse becomes the shared prose-tolerant extraction.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- All existing daemon repair tests stay green: `pnpm --dir packages/daemon exec vitest run tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts` (currently 55 passing).
- `parseOscarEvaluationArtifact` behavior is unchanged; its existing prose tests still pass.
- Add a regression test in packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts, mirroring the existing test 'surfaces founder escalation when Oscar wraps the evaluation JSON in prose' (~line 244): feed a prose-wrapped Deb turn output (an `applied` non-interfering .md self-fix, or a `proposal`) and assert the dialogue does NOT 500 — it returns status 200 and produces the expected artifact/outcome — instead of failing with 'malformed artifact'.
- Add/extend a unit test in packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts covering the shared helper extracting a Deb response object from prose-wrapped text.
- Do NOT duplicate the artifact contract or extraction in a second copy. Do NOT weaken any existing assertion. Touch only what this atom requires; preserve all unrelated code.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/oscar-deb-repair.ts, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts, packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts.

REPORT BACK: the full diff and the exact `pnpm --dir packages/daemon exec vitest run tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts` output showing all tests green.
